meaning: adj.不庄重的,无威严的,有损尊严的
examples: Such conduct is undignified for a person of his position.
There was an undignified scramble for the best seats.
She had to run about after him, though it was rather undignified.
The experts said performing CPR in inappropriate cases could result in a distressing and undignified death.
Meanwhile it is forcing Messrs Brown, Darling and Reid into undignified scrambling for new seats.
